page: Amazon Web Services r8i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix mathematics. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark testing, the r8i.4xlarge demonstrates strong single-threaded performance, scoring in the top 25% for Geekbench single-core (2019.0) and gzip compression (34.05 GB/sec). Text processing performance is highly efficient, reaching the top 10% tier with a Geekbench text processing score of 2904.0. For machine learning tasks, the hardware-assisted AMX instructions enable top-tier text generation speeds of 337.11 tokens per second on small LLM models. Multi-core operations, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance (17270.78) and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression (110.75 GB/sec), fall within the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 1277.8 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 120.82 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

The instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that require substantial RAM without scaling up vCPU counts excessively. However, the lack of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, which may introduce latency compared to instances with local SSDs. Additionally, its baseline network bandwidth is limited to 8 Gbps, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This profile is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, including in-memory databases like Redis, text processing pipelines, small-scale LLM inference, and general enterprise applications requiring high DDR5 memory capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 128.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). While the instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. Benchmarks highlight strong single-threaded capabilities, with top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation. This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, such as in-memory databases, text processing pipelines, and enterprise applications requiring dense memory configurations without excessive compute scaling.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 server featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and advanced Intel matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 480 MB L3 cache and AMX support
Top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation
No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for in-memory databases, text processing, and memory-bound applications
